speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Ah, there you are, [username]. Just in time — today we’re making something truly elegant: Lasooni Palak Paneer.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: First, we begin with blanching our spinach. A simple step, yet crucial for that vibrant emerald color.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Fill your pot with water, add a generous pinch of salt and a touch of baking soda. This ensures the spinach stays beautifully green.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Once the water comes to a roaring boil, add the chopped spinach, fresh green garlic, and that mild green chili.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Blanch for just a minute — no more. Overcooking will dull both the flavor and color, and we can't have that, can we, [username]?

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Quickly transfer everything into ice-cold water. This stops the cooking instantly and locks in freshness.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Strain, squeeze gently, and grind the spinach into a smooth, velvet-green paste.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Time to prepare our paneer. Heat oil and shallow-fry the cubes until both sides turn golden.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Once fried, transfer the paneer into warm water. This keeps it soft. A small trick… but essential.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Now for our aromatic masala — coriander seeds, peppercorns, cumin, and salt. Dry roast gently.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Crush it coarsely in a mortar. The aroma… quite irresistible, isn’t it, [username]?

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: For the main gravy, heat oil and drop in dry red chilies and cumin seeds. Let them splutter elegantly.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Add sliced garlic and sauté until lightly golden. Its perfume is the soul of this dish.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Now add the spinach paste. Stir slowly — almost like painting, [username].

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Add ghee, salt, and a pinch of sugar to balance the flavors. Let it all come together.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Adjust consistency with water, then add finely chopped coriander stems. A delicate but important detail.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: In goes more water, the roasted masala, and our softened paneer cubes.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Add diced tomato and let everything simmer gently. Patience, [username] — flavors need time to bloom.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: While that simmers, prepare a tadka. Heat oil, add a red chili, and chopped garlic. Sauté until fragrant.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: This tadka will crown our dish — bold, aromatic, irresistible.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Your Lasooni Palak Paneer is nearly ready. Give it one last, gentle stir.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Transfer it to a serving bowl — careful, it’s hot.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Add a swirl of fresh cream… beautifully done.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Now drizzle your garlic tadka on top. The aroma is divine, isn't it, [username]?

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Finish with a sprig of coriander. Simple, elegant, and complete.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: And there it is — Lasooni Palak Paneer. Vibrant, creamy, fragrant. You’ve done well, [username].

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: Serve it hot with roti or naan. Anyone you cook for will be impressed… naturally.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: You handled this beautifully.

speaker: Robin
dialogue_text: See you Soon [username]!.
